title: capparis spinosa subsp. inermis
intro: Capparis spinosa subsp. inermis, also known as the prickly ash or caper bush, is a subspecies of the caper plant (Capparis spinosa), which is a perennial shrub native to the Mediterranean region. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Capparis spinosa subsp. inermis include reducing inflammation and pain, as well as treating digestive issues and respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used as a spice or added to salads for its flavor and nutritional value.
